mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender 200Tdi engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 200Tdi engine typically costs £3,500–£4,500 supply only. Fitting adds £700–£1,000 depending on the specialist. Used engines can be found from £800–£2,000, but condition varies dramatically. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Defender.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Defender 200Tdi engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the 200Tdi is widely regarded as one of the most dependable Defender engines ever built. Many have exceeded 300,000 miles with proper maintenance. It's a simple, mechanical 2.5-litre turbo-diesel with no ECU — easy to work on and globally supported. The rubber timing belt is its only significant vulnerability.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Defender 200Tdi?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main issues are timing belt deterioration (requires replacement every 60k miles or 5 years), head gasket failure between cylinder four and the pushrod tubes, and injection pump/lift pump wear. All are well understood and repairable — the 200Tdi has no catastrophic design flaws.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender 200Tdi engine, or should I scrap the vehicle?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the vehicle's condition. Against a well-maintained £8,000–£12,000 Defender, a £4,000 recon is 33–50% of the car's value — often worth it. Against a heavily corroded example worth £3,000–£5,000, the same replacement may exceed our 60% Land Rover threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Defender 200Tdi engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is located on a flat surface at the top of the block, under injector number one. Defender-spec 200Tdi engines carry the 11L prefix. Discovery-spec engines are 12L (manual) or 13L (auto).

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 200Tdi timing belt, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The belt must be replaced — it's a rubber component with a finite service life. A preventative replacement at 60,000 miles or 5 years costs £500–£800. A snapped belt almost always bends pushrods and can crack pistons, requiring a £3,500–£4,500 engine replacement.

rows.whenItMakesSense: Every 60k miles or 5 years — non-negotiable maintenance
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Catch it early — straightforward repair on a well-maintained unit
Specialist rebuild is routine; far cheaper than replacement

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 200Tdi? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing belt change within last 60k miles or 5 years
* No signs of overheating or coolant loss (head gasket health)
* Clean cold start with minimal smoke
* A pre-1994 example with the 200Tdi — the last of the truly mechanical Defender diesels
avoidIf: * No timing belt history — assume it's overdue and budget £500–£800 immediately
* Overheating history or pressurised coolant system — head gasket may be failing
* Hard starting or significant black smoke — injection pump may be worn
* Excessive oil leaks around the rear of the engine — could indicate rear main seal or more serious issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 200Tdi is often considered one of the most dependable engines ever fitted to a Defender — a 2.5-litre mechanical turbo-diesel with no ECU and global parts availability. Its reputation is built on simplicity and torque, not refinement. The rubber timing belt is its one real vulnerability — maintain that and the 200Tdi will outlast the chassis around it.

cta.label: → Compare 200Tdi engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender 200Tdi (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for smooth start; any hesitation or excessive smoke points to injector or pump issues
* Service History — Including timing belt change records (critical — 60k/5-year interval) — see the map above
* Coolant System — Check for pressurisation, oil in coolant, or coolant loss (head gasket warning)
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lack of power or excessive black smoke?
* Oil Leaks — Check around the rear of the engine and timing cover
* Smoke Check — Blue smoke = oil burning; black smoke = over-fuelling; white smoke = coolant ingress
* Engine Warning Light — Not applicable — 200Tdi is fully mechanical, no ECU
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures and structural corrosion (Defender-specific)
